name: Thalamus
location: Diencephalon core surrounding the third ventricle
description: The grand relay station of the brain, gating almost all sensory modalities (except olfaction) en route to cerebral cortex and regulating consciousness.
functions: Sensory relay (LGN for vision, MGN for audition, VPL/VPM for somatosensory)
Thalamocortical rhythm generation and sleep spindle gating
Motor feedback routing from basal ganglia and cerebellum to motor cortex
Attention and arousal regulation via intralaminar nuclei
clinicalImportance: Thalamic lesions cause severe contralateral sensory loss, intractable pain syndromes, or coma if bilateral.
researchNotes: Thalamic reticular nucleus (TRN) acts as a attentional searchlight, selectively suppressing non-relevant sensory streams.
interestingFacts: Acts as the brain's central router, processing 99% of all incoming sensory data before cortex sees it.
